method_atom: A special-frame (light-cone-type) reduction that recasts the equations of motion of an extended, area-sweeping dynamical object first into the hydrodynamics of a compressible fluid with a particular negative-pressure equation of state, and then into a purely geometric variational problem: the static configurations are exactly the surfaces of vanishing extrinsic mean curvature, i.e. area-minimizing (minimal) surfaces solving the Plateau boundary-value problem. Explicit static solutions are then constructed by lifting known minimal surfaces across neighbouring dimensions, and the underlying constrained dynamics is organized within a constrained-Hamiltonian (gauge-symmetry, BRST) formalism.
object_atom: A bosonic d-brane (a d-dimensional relativistic membrane) propagating in a (d+1,1)-dimensional spacetime, whose light-cone-gauge description coincides with the Chaplygin-gas system, studied within string/field theory.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the reduction of an extended object's dynamics to a minimal-surface / Plateau variational problem plus constrained-Hamiltonian (BRST) structure, while the domain-specific object is the relativistic bosonic d-brane and its Chaplygin-gas realization in high-dimensional spacetime.
